reference: Hi, my eleven-month-old son had a febrile convulsion. He also has chickenpox. I have now noticed that on his skull, under his skin, he has two smooth lumps, one bigger than the other. He has been very tired and has had a temperature, but I have put this down to the chickenpox. Should I be worried about these lumps?
text: Hi, my 11-month-old son had a febrile convulsion; he also has chickenpox. I have now noticed that on his skull, under his skin, he has two smooth lumps, one bigger than the other. He has been very tired and has had a temperature, but I have put this down to the chickenpox. Should I be worried about these lumps?
